/azp run Azure.sonic-buildimage

Pulled in the build-infra fix from #28144 (author: @yijingyan2) as a temporary commit to unblock CI.

The shared `Build sonic image` stage was failing on **all** platforms with:
```
Cannot uninstall regex 2024.11.6 ... no RECORD file was found for regex
hint: The package was installed by debian
```
This is a master-side apt-vs-pip conflict (`python3-regex` installed via apt has no pip RECORD, so pip can't upgrade it when the sonic-utilities wheel pulls a newer regex) — unrelated to this PR's docker-sonic-vs changes.

The temp commit `ae6b414a4` is clearly labeled `[TEMP]` and will be **dropped on rebase once #28144 merges** into master.

Hi @sivat6, @prsunny, could you please help take a look at the test failures? It seems to be related to your changes in https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-sairedis/pull/1906. Thanks

> Hi @sivat6, @prsunny, could you please help take a look at the test failures? It seems to be related to your changes in [sonic-net/sonic-sairedis#1906](https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-sairedis/pull/1906). Thanks

I am looking into this. Looks like we are getting this error log.

2026 Jun 15 06:51:57.578508 vlab-01 ERR syncd#syncd: :- run: select failed: 2

I have added 1 sec timer on this select() in  Syncd::run(). 

int result = s->select(&sel, 1000);

This may be causing the failure. I am working on a fix.

@kperumalbfn , @yxieca , would you help merge this?
@lolyu please chime in.
@Janetxxx can you confirm the -w 0 behavior, is it wait forever or no wait?

> @Janetxxx can you confirm the -w 0 behavior, is it wait forever or no wait?

@yxieca `-w 0` means no deadline / no wait. Confirmed from upstream source: https://github.com/iputils/iputils/blob/master/arping.c#L134

> > @Janetxxx can you confirm the -w 0 behavior, is it wait forever or no wait?
> 
> @yxieca `-w 0` means no deadline / no wait. Confirmed from upstream source: https://github.com/iputils/iputils/blob/master/arping.c#L134

Can you test arping -w 0 a non-existing neighbor to confirm?

> Please confirm arping -w 0 behavior.

Hi @yxieca, I confirmed on a SONiC device
arping -w 0 returns immediately — it's no wait.
Test against a non-existing neighbor 10.0.0.130 on Ethernet520:
```
admin@str5-sn5640-5:~$  time timeout 15 sudo arping -w 0 -c 1 -I Ethernet520 10.0.0.130
ARPING 10.0.0.130

--- 10.0.0.130 statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 0 packets received, 100% unanswered (0 extra)


real    0m0.061s
user    0m0.005s
sys     0m0.016s
```

@Janetxxx  thanks for following up. On second thought I removed my approval.

Here is the discrepancy:

On sonic, we installed package "arpping", with this package, -w 0 indeed mean no wait and return immediately.

However, if we install "iputils-arping" (I tried this on Ubunto), -w 0 means wait forever.

This is a trap that we might fall into with unexpected behaviors. I suggest add a unit test or sonic-mgmt test to make sure that the arp we ar using will return immediately with unreachable IP.

Validated on Ubuntu, package 'arping' does return right away with -w 0 as well.
@yxieca Can we merge this?
